item.description:
# AI Usage Battery 0.2.3 (Build 7)
This patch release fixes account-switching usage bleed and reduces memory pressure during local Codex and Claude usage refreshes.
## Changes
- Limits Codex and Claude usage detection to activity recorded after the currently detected account credential was updated. - Stops reusing cached usage snapshots when the detected provider account id changes. - Removes archived Codex sessions from the default rate-limit scan path. - Avoids duplicate scanning when a configured source path already contains another source path. - Coalesces automatic menu-bar refresh triggers so SwiftUI appear/task events do not start overlapping scans. - Runs the local usage probe serially to avoid stacked helper processes during refresh bursts. - Streams Claude transcript usage into per-window totals instead of retaining a full event array during refresh. - Narrows Claude default transcript scanning to concrete usage sources instead of recursively walking the whole Claude config directory. - Replaces the Codex probe helper's broad recursive `rg` scan with bounded recent-file tail reads. - Keeps stale Claude statusline and probe cache output from applying after an account switch.
## Verification
- Swift test suite passes. - Probe helper shell syntax validates. - Fresh debug smoke run reduced measured physical footprint from a stale 1.0 GB debug process to 21.5 MB on the rebuilt app. - Xcode release build is expected to remain Developer ID signed, notarized, stapled, Gatekeeper assessed, and packaged with the existing verified DMG Finder layout flow.
